metadata.title:
CITY OWNERSHIP


GAINS A POINT
CAN CONDEMN PROPERTY OF
PRIVATE CORPORATION
NEEDED FOR A CITY


UTILITY.

pages.raw_text:
BY a margin of one vote the SUI
preme court of the state of Wash
ington has conceded to cities that
may hereafter desire to purchase
and operate their public utilities a
valuable point. 1t has given the
right of the city to condemn the
property of a private public utility
corporation when the same is
needed for the operation of a mu-
nicipal public utility. In other
words, this gives the municipality
owned utility precedence in the
power of condemnation over that
exercised by the privately owned
utility.


The case came up from Tacoma
in the effort of that city to con-
denim a power site for the purpose
of enlarging the municipal lighting
plant. The site was already held by
public utility corporation, which
contended that the city could not
take their property. Five judges of
the supreme court, Morris. Mount,
Parker, Crow and Chadwick upheld
the right of the city to condemn.
while Judges Rudkin, Fullerton,
Gose and Dunbar held the opposite
view. The case will probably go to
the supreme court of the United
States.
